summary: For LC-MS/MS data, data dependent acquisition (DDA) is the most used approach for generating fragmentation spectra.
In DDA, the top N most intense ions are selected for fragmentation before the cycle starts again.
The example DDA and SWATH data files are from reversed-phase LC-MS/MS runs of the Agilent Pesticide mix.
Most signal in the DDA dataset was measured between approximately 200 and 600 seconds.
In the DDA dataset, chromatographic peak detection identified peaks at specific m/z values.

CentWaveParam was used for chromatographic peak detection with snthresh of 5, noise of 100, ppm of 10, and peakwidth of 3 to 30. CentWaveParam with snthresh of 3, noise of 10, ppm of 10, and peakwidth of 3 to 30 was used for MS2 peak detect
The chromPeakSpectra method extracts MS2 spectra with precursor m/z and retention time within the range of chromatographic peaks.
The returned Spectra from chromPeakSpectra contains a chrom_peak_id spectra variable that identifies the associated chromatographic peak.
An example chromatographic peak of an ion with m/z 304.1131 was identified in the DDA dataset.
Flumazenil (Metlin ID 2724) has experimental MS2 spectra available in reference databases. Fenamiphos (Metlin ID 72445) has experimental MS2 spectra available in reference databases.
In the example, 5 MS2 spectra were associated with the candidate chromatographic peak.
The combineSpectra method can reduce multiple fragment spectra into a single consensus spectrum.
combinePeaks combines mass peaks from input spectra with m/z differences smaller than 20 ppm into one peak.
With minProp parameter set to 0.8, the resulting spectrum contains only peaks present in 80% of input spectra.
The example experimental spectrum matches Fenamiphos better than Flumanezil in similarity comparisons.
